Justification: The blender shall be powered by a single-speed motor with a minimum wattage of 300W, optimized for energy efficiency (target <15 Wh per blending cycle). A pulse function shall be included for controlled bursts.; The blade assembly shall be designed for tool-less detachment by the user, featuring a robust, simple, and reliable mechanism that prevents leaks and maintains durability. Blades shall be constructed from readily recyclable stainless steel, with rigorous testing if recycled material is used. The expected lifespan of the blades shall be defined, along with a clear process for their eventual recycling.; The blending jar shall be designed for ease of thorough cleaning using simple methods, avoiding overly complex geometries that significantly increase tooling costs or manufacturing complexity. If ABS is used for the jar, it must be a grade with proven impact resistance and long-term durability; PET or PP will be explored as alternative materials offering superior durability and recyclability without compromising performance or cost.

The maximum weight of the assembled blender shall not exceed 1.5 kg.; The housing shall be constructed from high-quality ABS plastic with a minimum of 30% post-consumer recycled content, validated for excellent long-term durability, impact resistance, and resistance to scratching or clouding. PET or PP will be investigated as potentially superior alternatives.; Integrated carrying features shall be incorporated into the housing or lid only if achievable without significantly increasing mold complexity, cost, or compromising structural integrity. A simple, robust, separate carrying accessory will be considered if integration proves challenging.

A Li-ion battery shall provide a minimum lifespan of 1000 charge cycles and a minimum of 15 blending cycles per charge (defined as 30 seconds of blending at high speed with the standard test mix). The battery pack shall be designed for ease of disassembly to facilitate recycling of its components.; The battery compartment must be designed for straightforward user replacement using common tools (standard Phillips head screws).; A USB-C charging port shall be utilized for universal compatibility and faster charging times.; The charging indicator shall clearly communicate charging status (charging, fully charged, low battery).; A robust and clearly communicated battery take-back or recycling program shall be implemented.

The motor housing and blending jar shall be constructed from materials offering excellent long-term durability, impact resistance, and resistance to scratching or clouding. ABS will be used if validated for these demanding applications; PET or PP will be prioritized if superior durability and recyclability are demonstrated without significant cost or performance penalties.; All major components will be designed for mono-materiality where feasible to facilitate recycling.; All materials in contact with food shall be BPA-free and phthalate-free.; The blades shall be made from durable stainless steel, designed for longevity and effective cutting.

All parts that come into contact with food shall be easily detachable and cleanable without specialized tools or excessive effort.; The detachable blade assembly mechanism must be secure, durable, and intuitive for users, avoiding glues or permanent bonding.; The motor base shall be wipeable and designed to prevent liquid ingress during cleaning.

Robust and reliable safety interlocks shall be implemented, designed for durability and potential replacement. Overly complex interlock mechanisms that can become failure points will be avoided.; Overheating protection for the motor shall be included, with an automatic shut-off mechanism designed for durability and potential user replacement.; All materials used shall be food-grade, BPA-free, and phthalate-free.

Target a minimum of 30% post-consumer recycled content for all plastic components (housing, jar, lid).; The product shall be designed for disassembly using accessible screws and robust snap-fits that can be undone without damage. Absolutely no glues or permanent adhesives shall be used anywhere in the product. The disassembly process for repair and recycling must be clearly documented.; All plastic components must be clearly labeled with their material type (e.g., ABS, PP).; Packaging shall be minimized, utilizing recycled and recyclable materials, and designed to reduce shipping volume and weight.; A clear battery take-back or recycling program shall be implemented.

The design shall be clean and simple, with ergonomic grips integrated into the main components. Ergonomic design choices shall not compromise durability, ease of cleaning, repairability, or disassembly.; Natural or low-VOC colorants shall be used, ensuring they do not hinder material recyclability. A limited, popular color palette (e.g., black, white, gray) will be offered to streamline manufacturing.; Functionality and sustainability will be prioritized in aesthetic decisions.
The blending jar capacity shall be between 350ml and 500ml.; The jar shall feature clearly marked volume indicators for accurate measurement.

Efficient motor and blade design shall be prioritized to minimize noise, aiming for below 85 dB during typical operation. Complex sound-dampening materials will be avoided.; If any sound-dampening elements are absolutely necessary, they must be easily separable and recyclable.

Motor longevity shall be rated for a minimum of 500 hours of use.; The battery shall be rated for a minimum of 500 charge cycles before significant capacity degradation.; Ice crushing capability: Ability to crush 5 standard ice cubes (approx. 20g each) with 50ml water in 30 seconds, without causing motor overstress, resulting in uniformly crushed ice particles. Rigorous testing of blade and motor durability under these conditions is required.; Blending time: Ability to achieve a smooth blend within 30-60 seconds for standard ingredients.

Strict adherence to a target retail price of $50-$100 USD is required. Value engineering will focus on optimizing material usage, manufacturing processes, and assembly to achieve this target without compromising product quality or lifespan.; Prioritize cost-effective sustainable materials with high recycled content, robust design, and essential repairability. Features that add significant cost with marginal user benefit or hinder sustainability will be omitted.

The battery compartment shall be designed for user replacement using standard screws. Common wearing parts (seals, blades) shall be available as affordable spare parts and designed for easy user replacement.; Clear, accessible repair and maintenance instructions shall be provided via a QR code on the product or packaging, linking to a dedicated website or online resources.; The product must be designed for disassembly and reassembly using common household tools to extend its useful life.
